original_text:
### [IMPORTANT] Icon Callout Box Rule A **light-blue accent-shape box** containing a short blue headline, with a **white icon badge that straddles the box's left edge** � the edge runs through the badge's center so it reads as half-on / half-off the box. Used for supporting "proof point" statements (dosing convenience, time-to-effect, etc.). **Layout (explicit):** - **Container** ? the callout sits on the page/section background (usually White `#ffffff`). The blue box itself is **inset from the left by half the badge width** so the badge's outer half has room � this is the "box gets a little smaller on the left" step. Everything else stays full content width. - **Box** ? Light Blue `#E6F0F9` fill, single accent shape. Vertically sized to its text with generous padding; the **left padding must clear the overhanging badge half plus a gap** (? `W/2 + 24px`). - **Text** ? Brand Blue `#00529b`, Nunito Sans weight 800, ~21px / 28px line-height, left-aligned, **vertically centered** to the box. Never let copy slide under the badge. - **Icon badge** ? a **White `#ffffff` rounded square** holding the line icon (Brand Blue stroke), **vertically centered** on the box and **horizontally centered on the box's left edge** (the edge is the badge's perpendicular bisector ? 50% over the blue, 50% over the page background). **Geometry (the part that makes it exact):** - Let `W` = badge width (use **80px**, icon ?48px inside). - Inset the blue box from the left by **`W/2` = 40px**, and place the badge so its **center lands exactly on the box's left edge** (badge `left:0` inside a `padding-left:40px` wrapper ? badge center `40px` = box edge). Distance from badge center to box edge must be **`0`**. - Vertical center via `top:50%` + `margin-top:?40px` (half the badge height). **Corner radius (mandatory, never change):** box `border-radius: 40px 8px 40px 8px;` in `TL TR BR BL` order � large on **top-left + bottom-right**, near-point on **top-right + bottom-left**, a **5:1 ratio**. The white badge **mirrors the same accent shape at smaller scale**, `border-radius: 20px 4px 20px 4px;` (also 5:1, large TL+BR). Never simplify either to a single uniform radius, swap the corners, or flip the diagonal. **Drop shadow (mandatory):** the white icon badge **must carry a subtle drop shadow** so it lifts off the blue box � e.g. `box-shadow: 0 4px 10px rgba(1, 30, 69, 0.18);` (Dark Navy at low opacity). This shadow is required on every instance; without it the badge looks flat and pasted-on. ## Palette reference | Token | HEX | Use here | |---|---|---| | Brand Blue | `#00529b` | Headline text + icon stroke | | Light Blue | `#E6F0F9` | Box fill | | White | `#ffffff` | Icon badge fill | | Dark Navy | `#011E45` | Drop-shadow color (low opacity) | ## MJML ```xml
These improvements occurred as early as four weeks after starting treatment, and they continued getting better through the end of the 52-week study.
``` ## Email fallback caveats (important) The 50/50 overhang relies on `position:absolute`, which Apple Mail / iOS / most web clients honor but **Outlook (desktop, Word engine) ignores** � there the badge drops to the top-left in normal flow and the box won't be inset. For pixel-perfect fidelity across **all** clients, the brand-safe option is to **pre-composite the white badge (with its shadow, accent radii, and icon) as a transparent PNG** and place it via the same absolute positioning, or bake the entire badge-on-edge treatment into the box's background image while keeping the text live. When that's not feasible, ship the live version above and accept the Outlook degrade (badge flush at the inner-left edge) � every other rule (Light Blue fill, `40px 8px 40px 8px` radius, blue 800 text, drop shadow) stays identical. ## Scaling note Defaults: badge size `W = 80px`, inner icon `48px`. If your real badge is a different size, keep the relationships � **inset = `W/2`**, **box left-padding = `W/2 + 24px`**, **badge `margin-top` = `?H/2`** � and the bisector stays exact.

relations.note: Geometry specifics refine the base structure.
Radius lock refines the base box/badge treatment.
Shadow requirement refines the badge treatment.
Fallback addresses limits of the absolute-positioned edge geometry.

Icon Callout Box Rule

rules.intent: Establish the canonical structure and treatment of the Icon Callout Box proof-point device.
rules.rule_text: The Icon Callout Box is a light-blue accent-shape box (icon_callout.box.fill = Light Blue #E6F0F9, single accent shape) containing a short headline in Brand Blue #00529b, Nunito Sans weight 800, ~21px/28px line-height, left-aligned and vertically centered. A white rounded-square icon badge (icon_callout.badge.fill = White #ffffff) holds a line icon with Brand Blue #00529b stroke. Used for supporting proof-point statements (dosing convenience, time-to-effect, etc.). Section padding icon_callout.section.padding = 16px 25px.

quotes: Let `W` = badge width (use **80px**, icon ?48px inside).
Inset the blue box from the left by **`W/2` = 40px**, and place the badge so its **center lands exactly on the box's left edge**
Vertical center via `top:50%` + `margin-top:?40px` (half the badge height).
inset = `W/2`**, **box left-padding = `W/2 + 24px`**, **badge `margin-top` = `?H/2`
Never let copy slide under the badge.
**horizontally centered on the box's left edge** (the edge is the badge's perpendicular bisector ? 50% over the blue, 50% over the page background)
Let `W` = badge width (use **80px**, icon ?48px inside).
Distance from badge center to box edge must be **`0`**.
keep the relationships � **inset = `W/2`**, **box left-padding = `W/2 + 24px`**, **badge `margin-top` = `?H/2`**

rules.intent: Fix the exact badge-on-edge geometry so the half-on/half-off proof-point device reads correctly.
rules.rule_text: The white icon badge (default W = 80px, inner icon 48px) must straddle the box's left edge with its center landing exactly on the edge: 50% over the blue box, 50% over the page background; distance from badge center to box edge must be 0. Inset the blue box from the left by W/2 = 40px, set box left-padding to W/2 + 24px, and vertically center the badge via top:50% + margin-top:-H/2 (-40px). Copy must never slide under the badge. If a different badge size is used, keep these relationships so the bisector stays exact.

quotes: box `border-radius: 40px 8px 40px 8px;` in `TL TR BR BL` order
`border-radius: 20px 4px 20px 4px;` (also 5:1, large TL+BR)
**Corner radius (mandatory, never change):** box `border-radius: 40px 8px 40px 8px;`
The white badge **mirrors the same accent shape at smaller scale**, `border-radius: 20px 4px 20px 4px;`
Never simplify either to a single uniform radius, swap the corners, or flip the diagonal.

rules.intent: Lock the signature asymmetric 5:1 accent-shape corners on box and badge.
rules.rule_text: Corner radius is mandatory and never changes: the box uses border-radius 40px 8px 40px 8px (TL TR BR BL) — large on top-left + bottom-right, near-point on top-right + bottom-left, a 5:1 ratio. The white badge mirrors the same accent shape at smaller scale, border-radius 20px 4px 20px 4px (also 5:1, large TL+BR). Never simplify either to a single uniform radius, swap the corners, or flip the diagonal.

quotes: `box-shadow: 0 4px 10px rgba(1, 30, 69, 0.18);` (Dark Navy at low opacity)
the white icon badge **must carry a subtle drop shadow** so it lifts off the blue box
This shadow is required on every instance; without it the badge looks flat and pasted-on.

rules.intent: Ensure the badge visually lifts off the box on every instance.
rules.rule_text: The white icon badge must carry a subtle drop shadow so it lifts off the blue box — box-shadow: 0 4px 10px rgba(1, 30, 69, 0.18) (Dark Navy #011E45 at low opacity, 0.18). This shadow is required on every instance; without it the badge looks flat and pasted-on.

**pre-composite the white badge (with its shadow, accent radii, and icon) as a transparent PNG**
every other rule (Light Blue fill, `40px 8px 40px 8px` radius, blue 800 text, drop shadow) stays identical

rules.intent: Preserve fidelity of the badge-on-edge device across email clients including Outlook.
rules.rule_text: The 50/50 overhang relies on position:absolute, which Apple Mail / iOS / most web clients honor but Outlook (desktop, Word engine) ignores — there the badge drops to top-left and the box won't be inset. For pixel-perfect fidelity across all clients, the brand-safe option is to pre-composite the white badge (with its shadow, accent radii, and icon) as a transparent PNG placed via the same absolute positioning, or bake the badge-on-edge treatment into the box's background image while keeping the text live. When that's not feasible, ship the live version and accept the Outlook degrade (badge flush at inner-left edge); every other rule (Light Blue fill, 40px 8px 40px 8px radius, blue 800 text, drop shadow) stays identical.

quotes: <div style="position:absolute; left:0; top:50%; margin-top:-40px; width:80px; height:80px; background-color:#ffffff; border-radius:20px 4px 20px 4px; box-shadow:0 4px 10px rgba(1,30,69,0.18); text-align:center; line-height:80px;">
